story.iWant: math operations drills that reinforce basic arithmetic
story.soThat: I build automaticity with addition, subtraction, and multiplication
story.tasks: - Create MathOperationsDrill component (src/features/training/drills/MathOperationsDrill.tsx) - Implement DrillProps interface (drill, onComplete callback) - Reuse NumberKeypad from src/shared/components/NumberKeypad.tsx - Create problem generator service (src/services/training/problemGenerator.ts) - Implement difficulty-based problem generation (easy: single-digit, medium: double-digit + multiplication, hard: 12×12 times tables) - Add visual feedback for correct/incorrect answers (green checkmark/red X) - Implement answer validation (max 4 digits, non-negative) - Add confidence prompt after each answer - Add accessibility features (aria-labels, screen reader announcements)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
**Given** a training session is active with Math Operations drill selected (Story 3.3 complete) **When** the MathOperationsDrill component renders **Then** the following behavior is observed:
**Problem Display:** - Math problem displayed prominently (e.g., "7 + 5 = ?", "12 × 8 = ?") - Font size: Large, readable (minimum 24px) - Problem type shown: "Addition", "Subtraction", or "Multiplication"
**Number Keypad:** - Reuse NumberKeypad component from Epic 2 - Buttons: 0-9, Clear, Submit - Clear button: Removes last digit - Submit button: Validates answer, triggers onComplete
**Difficulty Progression:** - Easy: Single-digit addition/subtraction (e.g., 3 + 4, 9 - 2) - Medium: Double-digit addition/subtraction + single-digit multiplication (e.g., 15 + 7, 18 - 5, 6 × 7) - Hard: 12×12 times tables only (e.g., 8 × 9, 12 × 7)
**Visual Feedback:** - Correct answer: Green checkmark ✓ animation, 1 second delay before next problem - Incorrect answer: Red X ✗ animation, show correct answer, 1.5 second delay
**Confidence Prompt:** - After each answer (correct or incorrect), show: "How confident were you?" with buttons: "Guessed", "Unsure", "Confident" - onComplete callback receives: { isCorrect, userAnswer, correctAnswer, confidence, timeSpent }
**And** Component integrates with TrainingSession drill queue **And** All buttons have 44px minimum tap target **And** Accessibility: aria-labels on keypad, screen reader announcements for correct/incorrect

doc.title: Epic 3: Training & Drill Engine Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Defines MathOperationsDrill.tsx implementing DrillProps interface with basic arithmetic (addition, subtraction, multiplication). Difficulty progression: easy (single-digit), medium (double-digit + multiplication), hard (12×12 times tables). Reuses NumberKeypad from Epic 2. Includes confidence prompts and telemetry recording.

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: All drills implement DrillProps interface { difficulty, sessionId, onComplete }. Each drill calls onComplete(result: DrillResult) to persist via telemetry service. Progressive difficulty tracks accuracy within session, escalates when >80% threshold met.

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Math Operations Drill: Keypad button press feedback <50ms. Reuse NumberKeypad component from Epic 2 (already optimized). Input validation: max 4 digits, non-negative integers only.

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.snippet: Uses shadcn/ui + Tailwind CSS v4 for mobile-first design. Feature-based architecture with drills in src/features/training/drills/. Shared components in src/shared/components/.

doc.title: UX Design Specification
doc.snippet: Headspace-inspired warm colors, circular comfort elements, delightful animations. Clean professional design with bright rounded aesthetics reduces math anxiety. WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility compliance required.

artifact.reason: Reusable numeric input component from Epic 2. Provides 0-9 digits, backspace, submit functionality with 60px+ touch targets. Must be reused in MathOperationsDrill per tech spec.
Reference drill implementation showing DrillProps pattern, visual feedback animations, onComplete callback, and Dexie result persistence pattern.
Reference drill showing confidence prompt implementation, difficulty-based problem generation, and Framer Motion feedback animations.
Session container that manages drill queue and renders individual drill components. Shows integration pattern for MathOperationsDrill.
Provides session state (sessionId, drillQueue, currentDrillIndex, results) consumed by all drill components.
Provides user preferences (soundEnabled, reducedMotion) for accessibility and feedback customization.
Dexie database instance for persisting drill results. DrillResult records stored in drillResults table.
TypeScript interface for drill result data shape. Includes operation type, problem string for Math Operations drill.

node.react: UI framework with hooks and concurrent rendering
node.framer-motion: Animations for correct/incorrect feedback, confidence prompt transitions
node.dexie: IndexedDB wrapper for persisting drill results
node.tailwindcss: CSS framework for styling (mobile-first, warm colors)

story-context.constraints: - MUST implement DrillProps interface { difficulty, sessionId, onComplete } - MUST reuse NumberKeypad component from src/shared/components/NumberKeypad.tsx (do not create new keypad) - Problem generator MUST be created as separate service: src/services/training/problemGenerator.ts - Difficulty progression MUST follow spec: easy (single-digit), medium (double-digit + multiplication), hard (12×12 times tables) - Visual feedback MUST use Framer Motion animations (green checkmark for correct, red X for incorrect) - MUST include confidence prompt after each answer with 3 options: "Guessed", "Unsure", "Confident" - Input validation: max 4 digits, non-negative integers only - Keypad button press feedback MUST be <50ms per performance requirements - All buttons MUST have minimum 44px tap target for accessibility - MUST add aria-labels to keypad buttons and screen reader announcements for correct/incorrect - Component MUST be located at src/features/training/drills/MathOperationsDrill.tsx - MUST respect UserSettingsContext.reducedMotion for animations - MUST call onComplete with DrillResult including operation type and problem string - Auto-advance timing: 1s delay after correct, 1.5s delay after incorrect (show correct answer)

interface.name: DrillProps
interface.signature: { difficulty: 'easy' | 'medium' | 'hard'; sessionId: number; onComplete: (result: DrillResult) => void; onSkip?: () => void }

interface.name: NumberKeypadProps
interface.signature: { value: string; onChange: (value: string) => void; onSubmit: () => void; maxDigits?: number; disabled?: boolean }

interface.name: DrillResult
interface.signature: { sessionId: number; drillType: 'number_line' | 'spatial_rotation' | 'math_operations'; difficulty: string; isCorrect: boolean; userAnswer: number | null; correctAnswer: number; confidence: string; timeSpent: number; operation?: 'addition' | 'subtraction' | 'multiplication'; problem?: string }

interface.name: Problem Generator Functions
interface.signature: generateAddition(difficulty: 'easy' | 'medium' | 'hard'): { problem: string; answer: number }; generateSubtraction(difficulty: 'easy' | 'medium' | 'hard'): { problem: string; answer: number }; generateMultiplication(difficulty: 'easy' | 'medium' | 'hard'): { problem: string; answer: number }

tests.standards: Use Vitest with React Testing Library for unit/integration tests. Use Playwright for E2E tests. Test files colocated with components (.test.tsx). Mock Framer Motion animations in tests. Use fake-indexeddb for Dexie mocking. Verify accessibility (ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, 44px tap targets). Test all three difficulty levels. Mock problem generator for deterministic testing.
tests.locations: - Unit tests: src/features/training/drills/MathOperationsDrill.test.tsx - Service tests: src/services/training/problemGenerator.test.ts - E2E tests: tests/e2e/training-drill-flow.spec.ts
tests.ideas: - AC: Component renders with problem display → Test: Render with difficulty='easy', verify problem text appears (e.g., "3 + 4 = ?") - AC: NumberKeypad reused from Epic 2 → Test: Verify NumberKeypad component is imported and rendered with correct props - AC: Easy difficulty generates single-digit problems → Test: Mock problemGenerator, verify generateAddition('easy') returns problems like "3 + 4" - AC: Medium difficulty includes double-digit and multiplication → Test: Verify generateAddition('medium') returns "15 + 7", generateMultiplication('medium') returns "6 × 7" - AC: Hard difficulty only 12×12 times tables → Test: Verify generateMultiplication('hard') only returns problems with factors 1-12 - AC: Correct answer shows green checkmark animation → Test: Enter correct answer, submit, verify green checkmark appears with Framer Motion animation - AC: Incorrect answer shows red X and correct answer → Test: Enter wrong answer, submit, verify red X appears and correct answer displays for 1.5s - AC: Confidence prompt appears after answer → Test: Submit answer, verify "How confident were you?" prompt with 3 buttons appears - AC: onComplete receives result with operation type → Test: Complete drill, verify onComplete called with { operation: 'addition', problem: '3 + 4', isCorrect: true, ... } - AC: Input validation max 4 digits → Test: Try entering 5th digit, verify NumberKeypad maxDigits=4 constraint enforced - AC: Accessibility aria-labels → Test: Verify keypad buttons have aria-label, screen reader announcements for feedback - E2E: Complete math drill in training session → Test: Start session, complete math operations drill, verify result recorded to Dexie drillResults table
